title: The Group

jokes.text: You know what l was gargling there? Was it Yankee Doodle Dandy? Yes. That was a good one.
jokes.setup: Larry is gargling and Cheryl guesses the tune
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of gargling recognizable melodies as a casual activity
jokes.explanation: Distinctly Larry David - turning mundane activities into inexplicably specific social interactions

jokes.text: Yeah, that way we can get rid of him.
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests going out for dinner with Uncle Nathan
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt admission that restaurants are escape routes from relatives
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's antisocial honesty with economical delivery

jokes.text: 'I'm a malingerer and he's a lingerer'
jokes.setup: Uncle Nathan is described as a lingerer
jokes.punchline: Larry identifies himself as a 'malingerer' creating a rhyming pair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that reveals character - Larry's self-awareness about avoiding work while criticizing others' annoying traits

jokes.text: 'He's leaving show business? That would be good news.'
jokes.setup: Jeff says he has good news to share
jokes.punchline: Larry suggests Jeff leaving the business would be the good news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - insulting his own manager while maintaining plausible deniability

jokes.text: 'Although I don't think he'd bill it as that'
jokes.setup: Larry just said Jeff leaving would be good news
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging Jeff probably wouldn't frame his departure as good news
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up that shows Larry's awareness of his own rudeness while not backing down

jokes.text: Long awkward silence after Cheryl asks why she always initiates sex
jokes.setup: Direct question about their sex life dynamic
jokes.punchline: Larry has no immediate response, creating uncomfortable pause
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb awkwardness - the silence is the joke, showing Larry's discomfort with direct intimacy questions

jokes.text: 'I got a cramp in my foot' as excuse for not responding about sex initiation
jokes.setup: Expected response about sex life dynamics
jokes.punchline: Completely deflects with minor physical complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry avoidance tactic - deflecting serious relationship talk with trivial complaints

jokes.text: Just assume that l want it all the time... so whenever you want it, just tap me on the shoulder.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his sexual availability philosophy
jokes.punchline: Reducing intimacy to a shoulder tap system
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Larry approach to solving relationship issues through mechanical systems - becomes recurring element

jokes.text: 'I will treat your breasts as if they're mine'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his aggressive approach to intimacy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre ownership metaphor for how he'd handle her body
jokes.explanation: Hilariously inappropriate and specific - captures Larry's complete misunderstanding of romance and boundaries

jokes.text: That's how l'll walk around. You know, l need borders, l need boundaries.
jokes.setup: Larry describing how he'd behave if constantly initiating
jokes.punchline: Visual of Larry walking around groping himself plus his need for 'borders'
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic need for personal space boundaries in ridiculous contexts

jokes.text: 'She's bringing The Vagina Monologues...' - Jeff's awkward phrasing
jokes.setup: Professional meeting about a theater opportunity
jokes.punchline: Jeff's discomfort with saying 'vagina' repeatedly creates awkward delivery
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness around the word 'vagina' - predictable but fits character

jokes.text: Cheryl's extended speechlessness at the opportunity
jokes.setup: Offered a professional theater role
jokes.punchline: So shocked she can't respond, creating awkward pause
jokes.explanation: Standard 'shocked into silence' beat - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: 'I've never actually seen the vagina with my glasses on'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry being supportive of the vagina play
jokes.punchline: Admits he's never clearly seen female anatomy due to not wearing glasses during intimacy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and specific Larry observation - the practical problems of nearsighted intimacy, delivered with complete matter-of-factness

jokes.text: Johnny Goldberg. Johnny Walker?
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests getting Jeff a bottle of Scotch
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion between Johnny Walker whiskey and a Jewish name
jokes.explanation: Quick cultural mix-up that shows Larry's Jewish frame of reference

jokes.text: 'Inside, I'm corroding' response to looking good
jokes.setup: Lucy compliments Larry on his appearance
jokes.punchline: Darkly honest response about internal decay despite external appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pessimism - turning a compliment into existential dread with poetic language

jokes.text: Look at you, getting some gray hair. lnside, l'm corroding.
jokes.setup: Lucy commenting on Larry's appearance
jokes.punchline: The dramatic metaphor of internal corrosion vs external aging
jokes.explanation: Poetically dark response to a compliment about aging gracefully

jokes.text: Look at you, getting some gray hair. -lnside, l'm corroding.
jokes.setup: Lucy compliments Larry on his distinguished gray hair
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatically dark internal assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - turning compliment into existential dread with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Lucy's casual admission about being a drug addict
jokes.setup: Asked what she's doing with her life
jokes.punchline: Casually announces drug addiction like it's a career update
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from treating serious addiction as casual small talk

jokes.text: Lucy's agent 'dropping the ball' on Vagina Monologues audition
jokes.setup: Lucy explains why she couldn't get an audition
jokes.punchline: Sets up later revelation that Jeff got her the role anyway
jokes.explanation: Setup for later revelation - works better in context of full episode

jokes.text: 'When he stopped actually being fun, I stopped actually liking him'
jokes.setup: Explaining why their relationship ended
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Sharp and honest relationship post-mortem with perfect economy

jokes.text: 'We don't have a child, do we?' - Larry's paternity panic
jokes.setup: Lucy wants to discuss something serious from their past
jokes.punchline: Larry jumps to worst possible conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neurosis - immediately catastrophizing the unknown serious topic

jokes.text: Larry literally tapping Cheryl on the shoulder for sex
jokes.setup: Earlier established shoulder-tapping system
jokes.punchline: Larry actually implements the ridiculous system
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution - Larry following through on his absurd proposal

jokes.text: 'I'm kind of tapped out' - Larry's masturbation confession
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking why he can't perform when willing earlier
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'tapped' - both exhausted and the physical action
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that works on multiple levels while revealing embarrassing truth

jokes.text: Lucy made 'a brief appearance and said goodbye' in Larry's fantasy
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking if he thought about Lucy while masturbating
jokes.punchline: Minimizing Lucy's role like she was a brief cameo in his fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor treating sexual fantasy like a polite social interaction - very Larry

jokes.text: Larry's protective attitude toward Cheryl not being 'defiled' in his fantasies
jokes.setup: Cheryl wondering why she's not included in his fantasies
jokes.punchline: Larry frames his mental infidelity as protecting her honor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - turning his mental cheating into chivalrous protection

jokes.text: l can degrade and defile her.
jokes.setup: Explaining the difference between wife and ex-girlfriend in fantasies
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual cruelty toward ex-girlfriends in his mind
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny insight into Larry's twisted moral code about fantasy ethics

jokes.text: She's got a bad attitude, doesn't take part in lesbian scenes... very hard to get along with. She's like a prima donna.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why Lucy is banned from fantasies
jokes.punchline: Treating fantasy women like difficult actresses with contract disputes
jokes.explanation: Masterful meta-joke treating fantasy life like film production with union issues

jokes.text: 'I'm gonna bring back the young Sophia Loren'
jokes.setup: Agreeing to eliminate ex-girlfriends from fantasies
jokes.punchline: Replacing them with classic movie star, treating fantasies like casting decisions
jokes.explanation: Good resolution that maintains the casting director metaphor while choosing universally acceptable fantasy

jokes.text: That's one of those things you go.... Wow! Blue Label!
jokes.setup: Jeff receiving expensive Scotch from Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-the-top appreciation for premium alcohol
jokes.explanation: Standard character beat showing Jeff's materialistic appreciation

jokes.text: Asteroid, hurricane, same thing.
jokes.setup: Lucy correcting the type of disaster movie
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive attitude toward disaster movie distinctions
jokes.explanation: Typical Hollywood agent dismissiveness of genre distinctions

jokes.text: 'You're just a big bowl of wrong'
jokes.setup: Context unclear but appears to be Jeff's reaction to something
jokes.punchline: Unusual insult construction mixing 'bowl of' with moral judgment
jokes.explanation: Quirky insult format that's memorable but not brilliant

jokes.text: l didn't know there was a prom. l had no idea that a prom even existed.
jokes.setup: Discussion about high school experiences
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete obliviousness to basic high school institutions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - missing obvious social experiences

jokes.text: 'I'm not gonna bring him' - referring to the stepfather perpetrator
jokes.setup: Larry asking who else she could bring to incest survivors group
jokes.punchline: Darkly noting she won't bring the actual perpetrator
jokes.explanation: Dark humor handled well - acknowledges the absurdity without making light of trauma

jokes.text: Are you sure that you qualify with your stepfather? ls that considered incest? No, if it's your stepfather, Larry, it's considered a date.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning the technical definition of incest
jokes.punchline: Lucy's sarcastic response about stepfather relationships
jokes.explanation: Brutally dark comeback that shows Lucy's defense mechanism against Larry's insensitivity

jokes.text: 'Sometimes when we're not together, I go to restaurants'
jokes.setup: Larry being found at restaurant with Lucy
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious as if it's a revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry deadpan - treating normal behavior as noteworthy discovery

jokes.text: 'We need our time alone' - awkward boundary setting
jokes.setup: Cheryl and Jeff wanting to join or stay
jokes.punchline: Lucy being inappropriately possessive of her lunch with Larry
jokes.explanation: Creates appropriate awkwardness for the situation without being exceptional

jokes.text: and he would make a 'Z' on my tummy with a feather... and then later, with his sword.
jokes.setup: Describing childhood abuse through Zorro roleplay
jokes.punchline: The progression from innocent feather to sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant use of childhood game metaphor for serious abuse

jokes.text: Uncles arguing during abuse about positions and technique
jokes.setup: Describing traumatic group assault
jokes.punchline: Perpetrators more focused on their disagreement than the crime
jokes.explanation: Very dark territory - the absurdist elements create uncomfortable comedy

jokes.text: lt was my stepbrother. Does a stepbrother count? lt's not really a blood relative. So, does it count? Step counts. Remember that everybody: Step counts.
jokes.setup: Same question Larry asked about stepfathers
jokes.punchline: Group leader's definitive ruling on step-relations
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that establishes group therapy's bureaucratic approach to trauma

jokes.text: This means that today l don't have to cut myself or put cigarettes out....
jokes.setup: Gwendolyn feeling good about sharing
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of self-harm alternatives
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mental health coping mechanisms in therapy context

jokes.text: Larry's defensive response about breaking up with Lucy
jokes.setup: Cheryl questioning how Larry could have dumped attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating acknowledgment of his dating limitations
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness from Larry about his perceived dating inadequacy

jokes.text: Right, yes, l know that's impossible... for an idiot like me to ever break up with a woman.
jokes.setup: Cheryl questioning if Larry broke up with Lucy
jokes.punchline: Larry's bitter self-awareness about his dating competence
jokes.explanation: Larry's defensive sarcasm about his romantic inadequacies

jokes.text: World without pillows philosophical tangent
jokes.setup: Random philosophical speculation
jokes.punchline: Treating pillow elimination as serious societal concern
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry random tangent that derails serious moments with absurd hypotheticals

jokes.text: Newsstand Dog Fancy magazine cover story
jokes.setup: Need to explain how they know each other
jokes.punchline: Absurd story about bonding over dog magazine reading
jokes.explanation: Decent improvisational lie that's specific enough to seem plausible

jokes.text: Cheryl's surprise at Larry's gambling problem
jokes.setup: Learning about Larry's supposed gambling addiction
jokes.punchline: Genuinely shocked by this fake revelation about her husband
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the lie's consequences - Cheryl believing fake problem

jokes.text: Jeff Greene as Lucy's manager revelation
jokes.setup: Jeff's earlier interest in Lucy's career
jokes.punchline: Confirmation that he got her the role Cheryl lost
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to Jeff's earlier transparent romantic/professional interest

jokes.text: Uncle Nathan's car accident story with fat man and jazzy woman
jokes.setup: Innocent story about helping at accident scene
jokes.punchline: Description perfectly matches Jeff and Lucy (drunk, fat, jazzy, convertible)
jokes.explanation: Excellent convergence - Uncle Nathan unknowingly describing Jeff and Lucy's accident

jokes.text: Big fat guy, and a woman, a real jazzy number. Both of them obviously drunk out of their minds.
jokes.setup: Nathan describing a car accident he stopped to help with
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing Jeff and Lucy's accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - audience realizes it's Jeff before characters do

jokes.text: Larry and Cheryl realizing Jeff was in the accident
jokes.setup: Uncle Nathan's description of accident victims
jokes.punchline: Recognition that he's describing Jeff and Lucy
jokes.explanation: Good payoff moment where the audience realizes the connection before or with the characters

jokes.text: You only have to talk about it. Not show it.
jokes.setup: Trying to calm Cheryl's nerves about the play
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate distinction about vagina presentation
jokes.explanation: Larry's attempt at reassurance becomes uncomfortable overshare

jokes.text: The osteopath? Yeah. How'd you know? Get out of here. What? Get out of here, you fucking piece of shit! Get out, you filthy animal!
jokes.setup: Uncle Nathan being introduced as an osteopath
jokes.punchline: Wendy's explosive reaction recognizing him as 'Todd's' abuser
jokes.explanation: Masterful culmination - Larry's improvised lie becomes horrific reality. Perfect Curb ending.

tmdb_overview: Larry runs into his old girlfriend, and she asks him to escort her to an incest-survivors meeting. Cheryl is asked to co-star in ""The Vagina Monologues"" when one of the actresses is sick.
